message:
fix(tools): resilient beads_rust binary resolution with version-probe fallback

Refactor BDAdapter to probe each candidate binary (br, bd) with
--version before trusting it, falling back to the next candidate when
the version command fails. Previously, resolveBinary() returned the
first binary found in PATH without verifying it actually works, which
caused silent failures when a broken or incompatible `br` binary
shadowed a working `bd`.

Key changes:
- Extract binaryCandidates() to deduplicate PATH resolution
- Add versionFromBinary() for isolated version probing per candidate
- Add workingBinary() that iterates candidates, returning the first
  one whose --version succeeds along with its parsed version
- Update Detect(), Version(), and runCommand() to use workingBinary()
- Replace manual os.Setenv/defer with t.Setenv() in tests for safety
- Add TestBDAdapterFallsBackToBdWhenBrVersionFails covering the new
  fallback path end-to-end (Detect, Version, GetStats all route to bd)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
